chunk_summary: Guidelines for managing supplement-related adverse events in adolescents, including stop rules, focused history, syndrome-based workup, and counseling on safety and risk elimination.
learning_objectives: Identify symptoms indicative of supplement-related adverse events in adolescents.
Understand the immediate stop rules and documentation process for suspected supplement harm.
Apply syndrome-based workup protocols for cardiovascular, hepatic, and other adverse effects.
Counsel families on the risks of supplement use and the importance of discontinuation.
key_facts: Immediate stop rule: Discontinue all nonessential supplements and document ingredients, batch, and proprietary blends.
Key symptoms: Palpitations, insomnia, tremor, hypertension, jaundice, dark urine, severe gastrointestinal symptoms.
Workup for palpitations: Check vitals, orthostatics, ECG, electrolytes, and glucose; escalate if QTc is prolonged.
Hepatic injury evaluation: Perform AST/ALT, bilirubin, INR, and metabolic panel; refer urgently for coagulopathy or encephalopathy.
Do not recommend rechallenge after suspected supplement-related harm, especially with liver injury.
Adulteration and mislabeling are common in sports supplements; do not assume label accuracy.
Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
reference_quote: Adulteration and mislabeling are recognized problems in sports supplements, so do not assume label accuracy. (Source: https://bjsm.bmj.com/content/early/2018/03/13/bjsports-2018-099027.alerts?versioned=true)
document_key_topics: Adverse events from supplements in adolescents
Stop rules and risk management strategies
Counseling on supplement safety and elimination
